(born 24 January 1990) is an English international indoor and outdoor [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") player. He reached a career high ranking of world number 17 in August 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

["BOWLS: Incomer Louis Ridout has outdoors Devon target after winning Planet Bowls title indoors"](http://www.torquayheraldexpress.co.uk/bowls-incomer-louis-ridout-outdoors-devon-target/story-25819091-detail/story.html). Torquay Herald Express.
["Commonwealth Games: Scotland's Alex Marshall wins record fifth gold as women take bronze in bowls"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/commonwealth-games/43748412). BBC Sport.
["KINGS SECURE FOURTH SUCCESSIVE MEN'S TOP CLUB TITLE"](https://www.bowlsengland.com/mens-top-club/). *Bowls England*.
["Commonwealth Games: England's men take gold in lawn bowls triples"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/commonwealth-games/62382397). BBC. Retrieved 2 August 2022.

["COMPETITORS CONFIRMED: WORLD BOWLS OUTDOOR CHAMPIONSHIPS 2023"](https://bowlsinternational.keypublishing.com/competitors-confirmed-world-bowls-outdoor-championships/). *Bowls International*. 5 June 2023. Retrieved 2 September 2023.
["Events and Results, World Championships 2023 Gold Coast, Australia"](https://www.worldbowls.com/events-list/). World Bowls. Retrieved 2 September 2023.

English bowls player (born 1990)

Louis Ridout

| Achievements and titles | |
| Highest world ranking | **17** (August 2024) |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Men's [lawn bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") | | | | Representing  [England](/wiki/England "England") | | | | [World Outdoor Championships](/wiki/World_Bowls_Championship "World Bowls Championship") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2023 Gold Coast](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship") | [triples](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Triples "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Triples") | | [Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Commonwealth_Games "Commonwealth Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2018 Gold Coast](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") | [fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") | | Gold medal – first place | [2022 Birmingham](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games") | [triples](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_triples "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's triples") | | Bronze medal – third place | [2022 Birmingham](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games") | [fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") | | [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | 2015 Paphos | pairs | | [British Isles Championships](/wiki/British_Isles_Bowls_Championships "British Isles Bowls Championships") | | | | Gold medal – first place | 2019 | singles | | Gold medal – first place | 2017 | pairs | | |

**Louis Ridout** (born 24 January 1990) is an English international indoor and outdoor [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") player. He reached a career high ranking of world number 17 in August 2024.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Bowls career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Ridout&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bowls career")]

In 2015 he won the pairs bronze medal at the [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 2016 he won the [National title](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_champions "List of Bowls England champions") in the Pairs with [Sam Tolchard](/wiki/Sam_Tolchard "Sam Tolchard").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The bowler who represents [Devon](/wiki/Devon "Devon")[[4]](#cite_note-4) represented England during the [2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2016_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2016 World Outdoor Bowls Championship").

He was selected as part of the [English team](/wiki/England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "England at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") for the [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") on the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland")[[5]](#cite_note-5) where he claimed a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal") in the [Fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") with [David Bolt](/wiki/David_Bolt_(bowls) "David Bolt (bowls)"), [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney") and [Sam Tolchard](/wiki/Sam_Tolchard "Sam Tolchard").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

He was crowned [National singles champion](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Men%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Men's National Championships") in August 2018 after defeating Andrew Squire 21–16 in the final.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He subsequently became the British singles champion after winning the [British Isles Bowls Championships](/wiki/British_Isles_Bowls_Championships "British Isles Bowls Championships") the following year.[[9]](#cite_note-9) He bowls for Kings BC, who have won the [Top Club championship](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Top_Club) "Bowls England National Championships (Top Club)") four years running from 2016 to 2019.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In 2022, he competed in the [men's triples](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_triples "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's triples") and the [men's fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") at the [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games").[[11]](#cite_note-11) The team of Ridout, [Nick Brett](/wiki/Nick_Brett "Nick Brett") and [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney") won the triples gold medal and in the fours he also secured a bronze medal.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In 2023, he was selected as part of the team to represent England at the [2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship").[[13]](#cite_note-13) He participated in the [men's triples](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Triples "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Triples") and the [men's fours](/wiki/2023_World_Outdoor_Bowls_Championship_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_Fours "2023 World Outdoor Bowls Championship – Men's Fours") events.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) In the triples with Nick Brett and [Jamie Walker](/wiki/Jamie_Walker_(bowls) "Jamie Walker (bowls)"), he won the bronze medal.
